memberdef.argsstring: )(struct rtio *r, const struct rtio_sqe *sqe, int res, void *arg0)
memberdef.name: rtio_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Callback signature for RTIO_OP_CALLBACK.

parameterdescription.para: RTIO context being used with the callback
Result of the previously linked submission.
Argument option as part of the sqe

briefdescription.para: Callback signature for RTIO_OP_AWAIT signaled.

parameterdescription.para: IODEV submission for the await op

briefdescription.para: The memory partition associated with all RTIO context information.
Get the mempool block size of the RTIO context.

simplesect.para: The size of each block in the context's mempool
0 if the context doesn't have a mempool

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio *r, size_t min_sz, size_t max_sz, uint8_t **buf, uint32_t *buf_len)
memberdef.name: rtio_block_pool_alloc

briefdescription.para: Count of acquirable submission queue events.

simplesect.para: Count of acquirable submission queue events

briefdescription.para: Acquire a single submission queue event if available.

parameterdescription.para: A valid submission queue event acquired from the submission queue

briefdescription.para: Acquire a number of submission queue events if available.

simplesect.para: The sqe array is in an undefined state if the return value is -ENOMEM

parameterdescription.para: Number of submission queue events to acquire
A pointer to an array of rtio_sqe struct pointers of size n

briefdescription.para: Drop all previously acquired sqe.
Acquire a complete queue event if available.
Produce a complete queue event if available.
Consume a single completion queue event if available.

detaileddescription.para: If a completion queue event is returned rtio_cq_release(r) must be called at some point to release the cqe spot for the cqe producer.

parameterdescription.para: A valid completion queue event consumed from the completion queue

briefdescription.para: Wait for and consume a single completion queue event.

detaileddescription.para: If a completion queue event is returned rtio_cq_release(r) must be called at some point to release the cqe spot for the cqe producer.

parameterdescription.para: A valid completion queue event consumed from the completion queue

briefdescription.para: Release consumed completion queue event.
Flush completion queue.

parameterdescription.para: if the queued operations completed with no error

briefdescription.para: Signal an AWAIT SQE.

detaileddescription.para: If the SQE is currently blocking execution, execution is unblocked. If the SQE is not currently blocking execution, The SQE will be skipped.

simplesect.para: To await the AWAIT SQE blocking execution, chain a nop or callback SQE before the await SQE.

briefdescription.para: Compute the CQE flags from the rtio_iodev_sqe entry.

simplesect.para: The value that should be set for the CQE's flags field.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct rtio *r, struct rtio_cqe *cqe, uint8_t **buff, uint32_t *buff_len)
memberdef.name: rtio_cqe_get_mempool_buffer

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the mempool buffer that was allocated for the CQE.

detaileddescription.para: If the RTIO context contains a memory pool, and the SQE was created by calling rtio_sqe_read_with_pool(), this function can be used to retrieve the memory associated with the read. Once processing is done, it should be released by calling rtio_release_buffer().

simplesect.para: -EINVAL if the buffer wasn't allocated for this cqe

briefdescription.para: Inform the executor of a submission completion with success.

detaileddescription.para: This may start the next asynchronous request if one is available.

briefdescription.para: Inform the executor of a submissions completion with error.

detaileddescription.para: This SHALL fail the remaining submissions in the chain.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio *r, int result, void *userdata, uint32_t flags)
memberdef.name: rtio_cqe_submit

briefdescription.para: Submit a completion queue event with a given result and userdata.

detaileddescription.para: Called by the executor to produce a completion queue event, no inherent locking is performed and this is not safe to do from multiple callers.

parameterdescription.para: Userdata to pass along to completion
Flags to use for the CEQ see RTIO_CQE_FLAG_*

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct rtio_iodev_sqe *iodev_sqe, uint32_t min_buf_len, uint32_t max_buf_len, uint8_t **buf, uint32_t *buf_len)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_rx_buf

briefdescription.para: Get the buffer associate with the RX submission.

parameterdescription.para: The minimum number of bytes needed for the operation
The maximum number of bytes needed for the operation
Where to store the pointer to the buffer
Where to store the size of the buffer

simplesect.para: 0 if buf and buf_len were successfully filled

briefdescription.para: Release memory that was allocated by the RTIO's memory pool.

detaileddescription.para: If the RTIO context was created by a call to RTIO_DEFINE_WITH_MEMPOOL(), then the cqe data might contain a buffer that's owned by the RTIO context. In those cases (if the read request was configured via rtio_sqe_read_with_pool()) the buffer must be returned back to the pool.
Call this function when processing is complete. This function will validate that the memory actually belongs to the RTIO context and will ignore invalid arguments.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the buffer to be released.
Number of bytes to free (will be rounded up to nearest memory block).

briefdescription.para: Grant access to an RTIO context to a user thread.
Revoke access to an RTIO context from a user thread.
Attempt to cancel an SQE.

detaileddescription.para: If possible (not currently executing), cancel an SQE and generate a failure with -ECANCELED result.

simplesect.para: 0 if the SQE was flagged for cancellation

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio *r, const struct rtio_sqe *sqes, struct rtio_sqe **handle, size_t sqe_count)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_copy_in_get_handles

briefdescription.para: Copy an array of SQEs into the queue and get resulting handles back.

detaileddescription.para: Copies one or more SQEs into the RTIO context and optionally returns their generated SQE handles. Handles can be used to cancel events via the rtio_sqe_cancel() call.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an array of SQEs
Optional pointer to rtio_sqe pointer to store the handle of the first generated SQE. Use NULL to ignore.
not enough room in the queue

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio *r, const struct rtio_sqe *sqes, size_t sqe_count)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_copy_in

briefdescription.para: Copy an array of SQEs into the queue.

detaileddescription.para: Useful if a batch of submissions is stored in ROM or RTIO is used from user mode where a copy must be made.
Partial copying is not done as chained SQEs need to be submitted as a whole set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an array of SQEs
not enough room in the queue

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio *r, struct rtio_cqe *cqes, size_t cqe_count, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: rtio_cqe_copy_out

briefdescription.para: Copy an array of CQEs from the queue.

detaileddescription.para: Copies from the RTIO context and its queue completion queue events, waiting for the given time period to gather the number of completions requested.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an array of SQEs
Timeout to wait for each completion event. Total wait time is potentially timeout*cqe_count at maximum.
Count of copied CQEs (0 to cqe_count)

briefdescription.para: Submit I/O requests to the underlying executor.

detaileddescription.para: Submits the queue of submission queue events to the executor. The executor will do the work of managing tasks representing each submission chain, freeing submission queue events when done, and producing completion queue events as submissions are completed.

simplesect.para: It is undefined behavior to have re-entrant calls to submit

parameterdescription.para: Number of submissions to wait for completion of.

briefdescription.para: Obtain an RTIO context from a pool.

parameterdescription.para: RTIO pool to acquire a context from
Valid context with permissions granted to the calling thread

briefdescription.para: Return an RTIO context to a pool.

parameterdescription.para: RTIO pool to return a context to
RTIO context to return to the pool

briefdescription.para: Prepare a nop (no op) submission.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_sqe *sqe, const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, int8_t prio, uint8_t *buf, uint32_t len, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_prep_read

briefdescription.para: Prepare a read op submission.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_sqe *sqe, const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, int8_t prio, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_prep_read_with_pool

briefdescription.para: Prepare a read op submission with context's mempool.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_sqe *sqe, const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, int8_t prio, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_prep_read_multishot

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_sqe *sqe, const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, int8_t prio, const uint8_t *buf, uint32_t len, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_prep_write

briefdescription.para: Prepare a write op submission.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_sqe *sqe, const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, int8_t prio, const uint8_t *tiny_write_data, uint8_t tiny_write_len, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_prep_tiny_write

briefdescription.para: Prepare a tiny write op submission.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike the normal write operation where the source buffer must outlive the call the tiny write data in this case is copied to the sqe. It must be tiny to fit within the specified size of a rtio_sqe.
This is useful in many scenarios with RTL logic where a write of the register to subsequently read must be done.

briefdescription.para: Prepare a callback op submission.

detaileddescription.para: A somewhat special operation in that it may only be done in kernel mode.
Used where general purpose logic is required in a queue of io operations to do transforms or logic.

briefdescription.para: Prepare a callback op submission that does not create a CQE.

detaileddescription.para: Similar to rtio_sqe_prep_callback, but the RTIO_SQE_NO_RESPONSE flag is set on the SQE to prevent the generation of a CQE upon completion.

This can be useful when the callback is the last operation in a sequence whose job is to clean up all the previous CQE's. Without RTIO_SQE_NO_RESPONSE the completion itself will result in a CQE that cannot be consumed in the callback.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_sqe *sqe, const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, int8_t prio, const uint8_t *tx_buf, uint8_t *rx_buf, uint32_t buf_len, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_prep_transceive

briefdescription.para: Prepare a transceive op submission.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_sqe *sqe, const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, int8_t prio, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_prep_await

briefdescription.para: Prepare an await op submission.

detaileddescription.para: The await operation will await the completion signal before the sqe completes.

If an rtio_iodev is provided then it will be blocked while awaiting. This facilitates a low-latency continuation of the rtio sequence, a sort of "critical section" during a bus operation if you will. Note that it is the responsibility of the rtio_iodev driver to properly block during the operation.
See rtio_sqe_prep_await_iodev for a helper, where an rtio_iodev is blocked. See rtio_sqe_prep_await_executor for a helper, where no rtio_iodev is blocked.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio_sqe *sqe, const struct rtio_iodev *iodev, int8_t prio, void *userdata)
memberdef.name: rtio_sqe_prep_await_iodev

briefdescription.para: Prepare an await op submission which blocks an rtio_iodev until completion.

detaileddescription.para: This variant can be useful if the await op is part of a sequence which must run within a tight time window as it effectively keeps the underlying bus locked while awaiting completion. Note that it is the responsibility of the rtio_iodev driver to properly block during the operation.
See rtio_sqe_prep_await for details. See rtio_sqe_prep_await_executor for a counterpart where no rtio_iodev is blocked.

briefdescription.para: Prepare an await op submission which completes the sqe after being signaled.

detaileddescription.para: This variant can be useful when the await op serves as a logical piece of a sequence without requirements for a low-latency continuation of the sequence upon completion, or if the await op is expected to take "a long time" to complete.
See rtio_sqe_prep_await for details. See rtio_sqe_prep_await_iodev for a counterpart where an rtio_iodev is blocked.

briefdescription.para: Get the next sqe in the transaction.

parameterdescription.para: if current sqe is last in transaction

briefdescription.para: Get the next sqe in the chain.

parameterdescription.para: if current sqe is last in chain

briefdescription.para: Get the next sqe in the chain or transaction.

parameterdescription.para: if current sqe is last in chain

briefdescription.para: Await an AWAIT SQE signal from RTIO IODEV.

detaileddescription.para: If the SQE is already signaled, the callback is called immediately. Otherwise the callback will be called once the AWAIT SQE is signaled.

parameterdescription.para: The IODEV SQE to await signaled
Callback called when SQE is signaled

briefdescription.para: Statically define and initialize an RTIO IODev.

memberdef.name: RTIO_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: Z_RTIO_SQE_POOL_DEFINE(CONCAT(name, _sqe_pool), sq_sz); \ Z_RTIO_CQE_POOL_DEFINE(CONCAT(name, _cqe_pool), cq_sz); \ Z_RTIO_DEFINE(name, &CONCAT(name, _sqe_pool), \ &CONCAT(name, _cqe_pool), NULL)

briefdescription.para: Statically define and initialize an RTIO context.

parameterdescription.para: Size of the submission queue entry pool
Size of the completion queue entry pool

briefdescription.para: Allocate to bss if available.

detaileddescription.para: If CONFIG_USERSPACE is selected, allocate to the rtio_partition bss. Maps to: K_APP_BMEM(rtio_partition) static
If CONFIG_USERSPACE is disabled, allocate as plain static: static

briefdescription.para: Allocate as initialized memory if available.

detaileddescription.para: If CONFIG_USERSPACE is selected, allocate to the rtio_partition init. Maps to: K_APP_DMEM(rtio_partition) static
If CONFIG_USERSPACE is disabled, allocate as plain static: static

memberdef.name: RTIO_DEFINE_WITH_MEMPOOL
memberdef.initializer: Z_RTIO_SQE_POOL_DEFINE(name##_sqe_pool, sq_sz); \ Z_RTIO_CQE_POOL_DEFINE(name##_cqe_pool, cq_sz); \ Z_RTIO_BLOCK_POOL_DEFINE(name##_block_pool, blk_size, num_blks, balign); \ Z_RTIO_DEFINE(name, &name##_sqe_pool, &name##_cqe_pool, &name##_block_pool)

briefdescription.para: Statically define and initialize an RTIO context.

parameterdescription.para: Size of the submission queue, must be power of 2
Size of the completion queue, must be power of 2
Number of blocks in the memory pool
The number of bytes in each block

memberdef.name: RTIO_POOL_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: LISTIFY(pool_sz, Z_RTIO_POOL_DEFINE_N, (;), name, sq_sz, cq_sz); \ static struct rtio *name##_contexts[] = { \ LISTIFY(pool_sz, Z_RTIO_POOL_REF_N, (,), name) \ }; \ ATOMIC_DEFINE(name##_used, pool_sz); \ STRUCT_SECTION_ITERABLE(rtio_pool, name) = { \ .pool_size = pool_sz, \ .contexts = name##_contexts, \ .used = name##_used, \ }

briefdescription.para: Statically define and initialize a pool of RTIO contexts.

parameterdescription.para: Number of RTIO contexts to allocate in the pool
Size of the submission queue entry pool per context
Size of the completion queue entry pool per context

briefdescription.para: Prepare a delay operation submission which completes after the given timeout.

detaileddescription.para: This operation will setup a kernel timer with the given timeout.

simplesect.para: Operation is enabled by default but may be disabled by turning off CONFIG_RTIO_OP_DELAY

parameterdescription.para: The k_timeout_t (e.g. K_MSEC(1)) to delay for
User supplied pointer to associated data

briefdescription.para: RTIO.
